transcript: Speaker A: You understand the gate announcements in English. You read the signs in English. But when the agent asks you a question, you freeze. I'm Loora. I'll be your check-in agent, your taxi driver, your hotel clerk. Every conversation you're scared of before you have it for real. Ten minutes a day. Want to try one now? Let's practice your check-in. Hello, where are you flying? Speaker B: I fly London. Speaker A: Do you mean "I am flying to London"? Speaker B: Yes, sorry. I am flying to London. Speaker A: Any luggage to check in? Speaker B: Luggage? Speaker A: Yes, luggage. It might be bags, suitcases and other containers people use to carry their belongings when traveling. Speaker B: Oh, I have one luggage. Sorry, I'm not so good English. Speaker A: Do you mean "My English isn't that good"? No worries. How many times have you spoken in English over the past month? Speaker B: Actually I didn't. Speaker A: So how do you expect to speak fluently? Speaker B: I not have anyone to practice with. Speaker A: Just download Loora. We'll speak together daily and you'll be fluent in no time. Speaker B: That's amazing. I'm adding this to my daily routine today.

summary: The ad targets English learners who understand the language but struggle to speak. It showcases Loora, an AI companion that simulates real-life conversations and corrects grammar in real-time. The call to action encourages downloading the app for daily practice.
